message:
chore(deps): bump vendored fuser 0.16.0 -> 0.17.0 + refresh Cargo.lock

Upgrades the vendored `fuser` crate from 0.16.0 to 0.17.0, the
workspace constraint that feeds it, and the companion lockfile
state. The abi-7-31 feature is preserved so the FUSE protocol
surface the ffs-fuse crate compiles against stays on kernel 5.2+.

Cargo.toml:
- Workspace dependency pin: `fuser = { version = "0.16.0",
  features = ["abi-7-31"] }` -> `fuser = { version = "0.17.0",
  features = ["abi-7-31"] }`. No other workspace pins changed.

vendor/fuser/Cargo.toml and vendor/fuser/Cargo.toml.orig:
- Both vendored manifests updated from `version = "0.16.0"` to
  `version = "0.17.0"` so the vendor copy matches the crates.io
  release referenced by the new workspace pin. All other metadata
  (edition = "2024", rust-version = "1.85", authors, build script
  wiring, license, homepage, etc.) untouched.

Cargo.lock:
- `fuser` lockfile entry version tracked to `0.17.0`; dependency
  list unchanged (libc, log, ...).
- Brings in `itertools 0.14.0` as a second registered major version
  alongside `itertools 0.13.0` (pulled by a pre-existing
  transitive). Cargo now needs to disambiguate existing consumers:
  `criterion` and `criterion-plot` dependency rows are re-pinned to
  `itertools 0.13.0`, while the downstream consumer tied to
  checksum `8a56d...` moves to `itertools 0.14.0`. This is purely
  disambiguation; no consumer requests a new major on its own.

No production code touched, no features added or removed. The
vendored-path guarantees remain identical because the feature set
requested by the workspace (`abi-7-31`) is carried through
unchanged. Rebuilds against the upstream 0.17.0 surface; if a
0.17-only API shift affects call sites, that will surface as a
follow-up commit.

files.patch:
@@ -493,7 +493,7 @@ dependencies = [
  "ciborium",
  "clap",
  "criterion-plot",
- "itertools",
+ "itertools 0.13.0",
  "num-traits",
  "oorandom",
  "page_size",
@@ -513,7 +513,7 @@ source = "registry+https://github.com/rust-lang/crates.io-index"
 checksum = "d8d80a2f4f5b554395e47b5d8305bc3d27813bacb73493eb1001e8f76dae29ea"
 dependencies = [
  "cast",
- "itertools",
+ "itertools 0.13.0",
 ]
 
 [[package]]
@@ -1216,7 +1216,7 @@ dependencies = [
 
 [[package]]
 name = "fuser"
-version = "0.16.0"
+version = "0.17.0"
 dependencies = [
  "libc",
  "log",

  "either",
 ]

+name = "itertools"
+version = "0.14.0"
+source = "registry+https://github.com/rust-lang/crates.io-index"
+checksum = "2b192c782037fadd9cfa75548310488aabdbf3d2da73885b31bd0abd03351285"
+dependencies = [
+ "either",
+]
+
 [[package]]
 name = "itoa"
 version = "1.0.18"
@@ -1781,7 +1790,7 @@ source = "registry+https://github.com/rust-lang/crates.io-index"
 checksum = "8a56d757972c98b346a9b766e3f02746cde6dd1cd1d1d563472929fdd74bec4d"
 dependencies = [
  "anyhow",
- "itertools",
+ "itertools 0.14.0",
  "proc-macro2",
  "quote",
  "syn",

@@ -13,7 +13,7 @@
 edition = "2024"
 rust-version = "1.85"
 name = "fuser"
-version = "0.16.0"
+version = "0.17.0"
 authors = ["Christopher Berner <me@cberner.com>"]
 build = "build.rs"
 autolib = false

@@ -5,7 +5,7 @@ license = "MIT"
 repository = "https://github.com/cberner/fuser"
 documentation = "https://docs.rs/fuser"
 homepage = "https://github.com/cberner/fuser"
-version = "0.16.0"
+version = "0.17.0"
 edition = "2024"
 rust-version = "1.85"
 readme = "README.md"
